15MIN INCREMENTS
03
04

Scripts: Module 3
SCRIPTS
TO REVEAL THE INTRIGUE
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
REVEAL
THE
INTRIGUE
This transaction is a little different than some you might
be familiar with. We have a pool of 5 investors already
in the deal, and all decisions need to be unanimously
by all the investors. You would be one of those decision
makers. So I have to carefully screen who we add to the
investor pool, because we need all parties focused on
the same financial outcome.
The pitch approach is
different, and you need to
prime the reception of this
message.
Try to use language
like “screen,” “select,”
“choose,” so that you
frame their participation as
not simply their decision,
but yours as well.
It is highly recommended
that you practice these
phrases with a friend or
colleague in order to get
the tone right before you
deploy them in a higher
stakes situation.
Whenever possible frame
the “you have to apply for
it” script as something that
is outside your agency.
That way, it is not you, the
seller, who is setting up
these rules, but it is just
the buying process that
you are a part of.
You Have To Apply for It
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
REVEAL
THE
INTRIGUE
You can’t buy our advanced product ... yet. There are lots
of companies that won’t sell you their most advanced
products until you demonstrate proficiency with their
basic product. It’s just like snowboarding school. You’ve
got to demonstrate that you can get down the bunny
slope, then the blue slopes, and only then do you
advance to rail park and get to do jumps. Our products
are very similar. You have to use our basic installation
and work up to the more sophisticated products over a
use period.
One of the common
approaches that is
recommended is to find
easily mappable metaphors
or social constructs to fit
your process into.
This technique frames
the discussion in familiar
and easily understood
constructs, so that your
requests don’t seem
outlandish or demanding.
Remember the key
principles of STRONG:
-people want what they
can’t have
-people chase what moves
away from them
-and people only value
that which they pay for
Set expectations by
putting a time constraint.
There’s More You Haven’t Seen
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
REVEAL
THE
INTRIGUE
We care about our relationship with our customers.
And right now, there are some things that we like about
you, and some things we don’t. We’re just not there yet
in terms of doing a deal together. Look, just because
someone is a willing buyer doesn’t mean we jump into
a relationship. This is the idea of counterparty. Let’s say
we sold MRI machines at $1 million each. If you wanted
to order 100, we would have to order $20 million of
supplies to support the order. Then, if you don’t perform
on the contract or change the order, or can you imagine
the $20 million pain in the ass that would be. In a similar
way, we have to apply this notion of counterparty to our
business. We need very good buyers, and I think we’re at
the phase where you are assessing us, but we are also
assessing you.
Signal your high status.
Just because someone has
money does not mean they
have higher status than
you. Remember that you
have the deal.
Say this. You need to
communicate that you only
take buyers who behave
themselves and reflect
your own good values.
When you’re setting up
these points of counter
pressure, always frame
them within a positive and
mutually beneficial light
Counterparty
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
REVEAL
THE
INTRIGUE
The plan we have just presented you is based on
conservative assumptions. It’s limited in scope on
purpose, because we like to give our investors and
partners a sense of certainty, that we will hit these
revenue projections. These are the numbers we are
confident in. And I know some people like to leave it at
that. They don’t want to see the possibility of the upside
numbers. They just want to stay focused on what is most
likely to happen. This is why I don’t show the Upside Plan
unless requested. The Upside Plan shows our model of
what happens when we do better than expected. It’s
the model that shows if customers buy 20% more than
expected, and our material costs go down slightly, how
much additional we’ll make for the financial Quarter.
I have this Upside Model prepared in this envelope. If
you were interested, we could take a break now and
come back together and break the seal on it and look
at it together. Otherwise, we’ll just continue as we have
been, reviewing the conservative plan.
Investors and buyers hear
unrealistically optimistic
projections 24 hours a day.
Stand out and raise your
own credibility by knowing
the playing field that
they operate in, and sub
communicating that you
“get it.”
Set up situations whereby
the buyer must express his
own interest in it, without
your having to ask for his
interest. Note the complete
absence of questions.
This is a point where
you are inviting them to
signal their interest. Give
people a way out. “I can
show you the exciting side
only by special request.”
If someone requests it,
you know that they are
interested.
Certainty
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 5 --
